novel_features.name: Multi-city/category agenda
novel_features.description: One flat, date-sorted list of upcoming events across multiple cities and categories at once.
novel_features.rationale: The API lists one place OR one category per call with cursor paging; we union across places and categories in the local store, de-page, and date-sort.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when the user asks 'what AI events are on this week' across several cities — no single Luma call can answer it.

novel_features.name: Geo-radius event search
novel_features.description: Find events within N km of a lat/lng, ranked by distance.
novel_features.rationale: The API exposes each event's coordinate but has no radius filter; we run haversine over the local SQLite store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the user wants events near a point or venue, which no Luma API call supports.

novel_features.name: ICS export of any filtered set
novel_features.description: Export a synced/filtered set of events to a .ics calendar file your calendar app can import.
novel_features.rationale: The API returns JSON only; we serialize any query result (a city, a category, a window) to RFC5545 VEVENTs, not just one Luma calendar.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the user wants events on their own calendar instead of re-reading JSON.

novel_features.name: Drift / new-since-last-sync
novel_features.description: Show what changed on synced events since the previous sync — new, removed, filling up, sold out, rescheduled.
novel_features.rationale: The API is stateless; we snapshot guest_count, ticket_count, sold_out, and start_at to SQLite across syncs and diff them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to monitor events over time; a single API call can never show change.

novel_features.name: Multi-calendar aggregate compare
novel_features.description: Side-by-side upcoming-event counts and total guest counts across several calendars (communities).
novel_features.rationale: The API is per-calendar; we GROUP BY calendar_api_id over local events to compare communities the API never aggregates.
novel_features.example: luma-pp-cli calendars compare cal-AAA cal-BBB cal-CCC --window 14d --agent
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to benchmark which communities are most active or drawing the biggest crowds.

narrative.headline: Browse, search, and export public Luma events from one Go binary — no account, no API key, plus offline full-text search the public API doesn't have.
narrative.value_prop: luma reads Luma's public discovery surface (api.luma.com) to list events by city and category, fetch full event and calendar details, and mirror it all into a local SQLite store. On top of the raw API it adds what Luma never exposed publicly: full-text search, geo-radius lookup, cross-city aggregation, change tracking, and ICS export — all agent-native with --json and --select.
narrative.auth_narrative: No account or API key required. luma reads Luma's public, read-only discovery API the website itself uses. It cannot create events, manage guests, or read private/subscribed calendars — that is the paid Luma Plus API's job.

quickstart.comment: health check — confirms the public API is reachable
list topic categories (AI, Crypto, Tech) and their ids
events for a category id from the list above
mirror a city's events locally so search/digest/drift work offline
offline full-text search across everything synced

troubleshoots.symptom: events near / search / digest / drift return nothing
troubleshoots.fix: Run sync first (e.g. luma-pp-cli sync --resources events --resource-param events:slug=sf) — these commands read the local store.
Use a real Luma city slug (sf, nyc, miami, london); run luma-pp-cli discover home to see places.
Lower --limit or add --max-scan-pages; the public API throttles aggressive paging.

narrative.when_to_use: Use luma when an agent needs to discover or report on public Luma events — what's happening in a city, events for a topic, full details for an event or community, or a calendar export. It is ideal for read-only event discovery, aggregation across cities, and tracking how events fill up over time.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI to create, edit, or cancel events — that needs the paid Luma Plus API.
Do not use it to manage guest lists, check-ins, RSVPs, or ticketing.
Do not use it to read private or subscriber-only calendars; it only sees public discovery data.

recipes.title: Event detail, narrowed for an agent
recipes.explanation: Event detail is large and deeply nested; --select pulls only the fields you need so an agent doesn't burn context.

recipes.title: AI events across three cities this week
recipes.explanation: Unions several cities filtered to a category into one flat, date-sorted list the API can't return.

recipes.title: Events near a point
recipes.explanation: Haversine radius search over synced event coordinates — no Luma endpoint offers this.

recipes.title: Export a city to your calendar
recipes.explanation: Writes an ICS file you can import into any calendar app.

trigger_phrases: what's happening in nyc on luma
